incident_report:
# Incident Report: Dungeon Agent Run (Seed 38)

This run failed to achieve any progress before hitting the 50-turn limit at turn 51, with both Agent A and Agent B unable to navigate the dungeon environment. The critical failure point was the pervasive occurrence of spurious_block anomalies that repeatedly interrupted movement attempts: Agent B encountered blocking anomalies on turns 2, 16, 24, 27, 40, and 45, while Agent A experienced them on turns 11, 14, 39, 41, 44, and 47. These anomalies were not isolated incidents but rather a systemic pattern that prevented the agents from making meaningful progress toward key objectives like picking up items or unlocking doors. The agents never communicated via messages or sought DM intervention despite the mounting obstacles, suggesting either insufficient anomaly-response protocols or a decision to continue pushing forward through blocked paths rather than adapting strategy. Without ever securing a key pickup or unlocking any doors, the agents exhausted the turn budget in what appears to be a highly anomaly-prone dungeon configuration where movement reliability was fundamentally compromised from the earliest turns.
